manufacturer_evidence: The Easy Wind is new to the Christenson model line up as of 2025. This model comes with a low rocker paired with a broad nose outline that provides ease of paddling and quick take offs. The bottom contour features a combination of double concave and forward vee which makes this board very responsive for its broad outline in a variety of wave conditions. The Easy Wind comes as a 5 fin setup to be ridden as a thruster or quad. We recommend using the Christenson 5 fin sets in either FCS2 or Futures.
rocker_notes: This model comes with a low rocker paired with a broad nose outline that provides ease of paddling and quick take offs.

manufacturer_evidence: The Lane Splitter is a full throttle twin fin that can push the limits. This model has proven itself in the line up from high tide mushy beach breaks to draining reefs in Indonesia. Just step on the gas and bang it thru all the gears. A great travel companion for a pro or an amateur. The recommended length is 2-4 inches shorter than your height. Made as a squash tail unless requested as swallow tail. Lane Splitter Squash Tail

manufacturer_evidence: Christenson describes the OP4 as a high-performance round-tail board for stronger surf when a full step-up is not required. The official model page identifies continuous rocker and outline curve for late drops, pocket surfing and direction changes at speed, and recommends riding it one to two inches longer than the surfer's height.

manufacturer_evidence: Osprey has 5 different fin options: thruster, quad, twin, 3 fin bonzer, and single fin. The most recommended is the 5 fin box configuration so the board can be ridden as a quad or a thruster to handle all conditions. The twin fin is recommended for the surfer that prefers a much looser board with less drag. The 3 fin bonzer is recommended for the surfer who prefers long drawn out turns, hi-lines, and point breaks. The single fin is recommended for the surfer that likes to keep it more casual and down the line with less emphasis on using the rail.
rocker_notes: The single fin is recommended for the surfer that likes to keep it more casual and down the line with less emphasis on using the rail.

manufacturer_evidence: The Wolverine is the spicy twin fin version of the popular nautilus model. This is a favorite mid size model of our high level surfers. The rocker is moderate to handle larger surf at ease, complimented by a pulled in winged swallow tail, and twin fin set up, to shoot sparks off the rail in fun sized surf. This can also be used as a step up in your board bag. The recommended lengths are 6'2 to 7'0". The length has no correlation with riders height, it's more of a desired choice of sensation. Any length below 6'2, the Lane Splitter swallow tail is recommend.
rocker_notes: The rocker is moderate to handle larger surf at ease, complimented by a pulled in winged swallow tail, and twin fin set up, to shoot sparks off the rail in fun sized surf.
